title: ALTA Cloud Marketplace Registry v1
description: Schema for the public registry document served by GET /api/v1/registry. Backward compatible with the ALTA client contract; server-side rules are stricter than the client (e.g. lowercase-hex SHA-256). Unknown additive fields are permitted so future additive fields do not break validation.

registry.description: Registry-level metadata. 'version' is retained for backward compatibility with the demo contract and is decorative. 'schema_version' is an additive field the current client ignores.

version.description: Decorative; retained for demo backward compatibility.

schema_version.description: Additive; value is '1' for Registry API v1.

items.description: Current-public-release projection: at most one item per addon code.

item.description: A single published addon (one current public release).

deprecated.description: Additive v1 field; ignored by the current client.

published_at.description: Additive v1 field; ignored by the current client.

publisher.description: Additive v1 field; publisher display metadata (not signed).

dependency.description: Two backward-compatible forms: a plain code string, or an object with a code and an optional constraint.

constraint.description: Version constraint, e.g. >=1.0.0. Null or '*' means any.

artifact.description: Immutable artifact metadata. Required for every published cloud item.

url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL on the Marketplace-controlled host. Never a presigned object-storage URL.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex, exactly 64 chars. Stricter than the current client validator (which checks length only). Server-side strengthening; does not break client contract.

size.description: Artifact size in bytes; must be > 0.

signature.description: Ed25519 detached signature over the exact raw ZIP bytes.

value.description: Standard base64 of the 64-byte detached signature.

key_id.description: Stable public-key identifier, looked up in the client's trusted_keys.

payload_version.description: Additive v1 field; documents that raw ZIP bytes were signed. Ignored by the current client.
